item.title: Photo Credit
item.content:encoded:
Photo is from Wikipedia, here: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Aerial_view_of_Lake_Ouachita,_AR.png
Liscensed under Creative Commons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I didn't know 2894 existed before I wrote this, then after I read it I realized how eerily similar many parts of it sounded. Honestly though, I don't know what I could change short of cross referencing 2894.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's well written, but it's far too similar to SCP-2894 for my liking, even aspects of the containment attempts mirror it too closely. I'm not going to downvote it though, because the interview at the end adds an interesting twist to the article, but it isn't enough to warrant an upvote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like that idea! Maybe the Blakely Mountain Dam is a little larger than it appears, and houses the turbines underneath the structure to generate huge amounts of hydroelectric power? I think I'll write that in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really liked this.. A simple anomaly with big ramifications.

The Foundation should set up a bunch of big turbines under it. Getting rid of the water will be a problem, but having a perpetual motion machine in the meantime would help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While they may have a similar skeleton I'd say the meat of the articles is very different.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Shit.

I've never read 2894 before, I really didn't know it existed.

I actually got this idea from another failed article. Here's the Drafts and Critiques forum post where I explained where the idea came from:
http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-10055544/heart-of-ouachita#post-4131335

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This sounds very similar to SCP-2894 (pre-neutralisation) i.e. an underwater anomaly that is gonna flood Earth unless something is done.
